mission.title: Install and Configure vLLM
mission.description: vLLM is a high-throughput and memory-efficient inference and serving engine for large language models (LLMs). It is ideal for applications requiring fast and scalable LLM serving with support for various hardware configurations. Key trade-offs include the need for GPU resources for optimal performance and the complexity of managing distributed systems.

steps.title: Add Helm Repository
steps.description:
This step adds the vLLM Helm repository to your local Helm configuration, allowing you to install vLLM using Helm.

```bash

helm repo add vllm https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/releases/download/v0.18.0

```

> This command adds the vLLM repository for version 0.18.0.

steps.title: Install Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs)
steps.description:
This step installs the necessary Custom Resource Definitions for vLLM to function correctly in your Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/vllm-project/vllm/v0.18.0/deploy/crds/vllm.io_vllms_crd.yaml

```

> This command applies the CRDs required for vLLM.

steps.title: Install vLLM using Helm
steps.description:
This step installs the vLLM application in a specified namespace using Helm.

```bash

helm install vllm vllm/vllm --namespace vllm --create-namespace --version 0.18.0

```

> This command installs vLLM in the 'vllm' namespace.

steps.title: Create an Example Custom Resource
steps.description:
This step creates an example Custom Resource (CR) to deploy a model using vLLM.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: vllm.io/v1
kind: VLLM
metadata:
  name: example-vllm
spec:
  model: 'gpt-3'
  replicas: 2
EOF

```

> This command creates a sample vLLM resource for a GPT-3 model.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: vllm.io/v1
kind: VLLM
metadata:
  name: example-vllm
spec:
  model: 'gpt-3'
  replicas: 2
EOF

steps.title: Verify the Custom Resource is Ready
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the deployed vLLM instance to ensure it is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get vllm -n vllm

```

> This command lists the vLLM instances in the 'vllm' namespace.

uninstall.title: Uninstall vLLM
uninstall.description:
This step removes the vLLM installation and associated resources. Be aware that this will delete all data associated with the vLLM instances.

```bash

helm uninstall vllm --namespace vllm

kubectl delete namespace vllm

```

> This command uninstalls the vLLM release.
> This command deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade vLLM
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up any important data. This step will upgrade vLLM to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl drain <node-name> --ignore-daemonsets --delete-local-data

helm upgrade vllm vllm/vllm --namespace vllm --version 0.18.0

kubectl get vllm -n vllm

```

> Replace <node-name> with the name of the node to cordon.
> This command upgrades vLLM to version 0.18.0.
> This command verifies that the vLLM instances are running after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'vllm not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The vLLM release may not be installed or the namespace is incorrect.

**Fix:**
Ensure vLLM is installed with the correct namespace using 'helm list --namespace vllm'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to pull image'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified container image may not exist or is inaccessible.

**Fix:**
Check the image name and ensure it is available in the specified registry.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The vLLM pod may be failing due to misconfiguration or resource limits.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs using 'kubectl logs <pod-name>' and adjust resources as necessary.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster may not have enough resources allocated for the vLLM pods.

**Fix:**
Scale up your cluster or adjust the resource requests in the vLLM configuration.

versionNotes.changes: Introduced support for new model types and improved memory management features.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see output similar to 'NAME            READY   STATUS    RESTARTS   AGE' with your vLLM instance listed as '1/1' under the READY column.

  replicas: 2
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with sufficient resources and the necessary tools installed.
